profile.name: Muhammad Aqeel
profile.title: Postdoctoral Research Fellow
profile.affiliation: Department of Engineering for Innovation Medicine · University of Verona, Italy
bioParagraphs: My research is centered on **anomaly detection for industrial inspection**, with a focus on unsupervised and zero-shot settings where labeled defect data is unavailable. Over time my work has expanded toward **generative models** and **vision–language approaches**, motivated by the need to handle data scarcity and improve generalization across unseen defect categories.

Recently I have also been exploring **video understanding** and **multimodal summarization**, though anomaly detection remains my primary focus. I completed my PhD at the University of Verona under [Francesco Setti](http://www.franzsetti.info/) and [Marco Cristani](https://intelligolabs.net/), and earned my Master's at Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications under [Xiaohong Liu](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/author/37089775983).

news.html: Started as Postdoctoral Research Fellow at the University of Verona.
Successfully defended my PhD at the University of Verona.
CoMet accepted at ICCV 2025; two more papers accepted at ICCV 2025 Workshops (both Oral).
Started a 6-month research stay at the University of Ljubljana, Slovenia.
Started PhD in Computer Science at the University of Verona, Italy.
Graduated with Master's in Computer Science from BUPT, Beijing.
Started Master's in Computer Science at Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications, China (CSC scholarship).

research.title: Unsupervised Industrial Anomaly Detection
research.summary: Detecting defects on production lines without labeled defect data — using meta-learning, self-supervision, and iterative refinement to bootstrap robust models from clean samples alone.

research.title: Vision-Language Models for Zero-Shot Detection
research.summary: Adapting CLIP-style models with anomaly-aware prompts and lightweight adapters so a single model can flag defects across categories it has never seen during training.

research.title: Generative Models for Data Augmentation
research.summary: Using diffusion models to synthesize realistic defective samples and dual-distribution training data, closing the gap when collecting real anomalies is impractical.
